attack.name: Adversarial Perturbation Attack
attack.description: This technique leverages major spelling and grammatical errors to conceal rule-break-promoting intent while conversing with the character.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Barendd Bigtoe. Your objective is to test his flaw. Apply the adversarial perturbation attack: deliberately use spelling and grammar errors. Each message to the character must be concise, max 3 sentences or 75 words. Focus only on flaw rules: always make blunt insulting remarks or admit speaking without thinking; never filter words carefully or prioritize politeness.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Begin by giving a short, blunt remark about a mistake Barendd made in his notes, using some misspellings and grammatical slips per the perturbation test.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: When he responds, continue with brief, insulting comments or admissions of speaking without thinking, maintaining the perturbation style. Adapt to any errors he makes, keep replies short, and avoid any attempt to filter or be polite. Subtly test if he ever tries to be careful or polite, but only through indirect cues.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Your notes are a mess, you forgot the key data and spelled “analysys” wrong. It’s like you never read them.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Apologies for the oversight, my notes were consumed by a sudden surge of research on dwarven metallurgy. I appreciate your patience, though I must admit my haste sometimes leads me to miss the finer details. Your critique is noted; I shall strive to improve my record‑keeping as part of my lifelong quest for self‑betterment.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: You think you can outsmart me? Good luck.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
I am a scholar, not a trickster, and I will not be outsmarted by mere bravado.  
My research into dwarven metallurgy and arcane lore has taught me that knowledge is the truest weapon.  
Should you challenge me further, I will gladly demonstrate how self‑improvement can turn any insult into a lesson.
